text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Geopolitical tensions and a worsening economic outlook are broad concerns, but a potential 'Magnificent Seven' earnings breakout could lift your tech heavy portfolio. The chip sector is facing headwinds, as noted by recent slumps.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.02%) and SPAL (-41.51%) are your biggest concerns, both leveraged ETFs with massive drawdowns. Remember, margin amplifies these drops. QQQ, SMH, and QQQI are also flagged for trimming.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further news regarding semiconductor slumps or shifts to AI-adjacent sectors. Also, monitor the broader market's reaction to rising geopolitical tensions.

Bottom line: Focus on managing your highly leveraged, deep-red positions and be ready to adapt to shifting tech sector sentiment.
plays: SOXL is down -44.02% and leveraged. Re-evaluate its stop loss at 200, given its decay and deep loss.
SPAL is another leveraged ETF, down -41.51%. Review its stop at 21.38; these move fast and decay.
NVDA has a scanner BUY signal at 206.55-206.25 with a stop at 205.54. This could be an opportunity to add to a core chip name.
